name: Arthur Miller
abstract: Arthur Asher Miller (October 17, 1915 – February 10, 2005) was an American playwright and essayist in the 20th-century American theater. Among his most popular plays are All My Sons (1947), Death of a Salesman (1949), The Crucible (1953), and A View from the Bridge (1955).

about_quotes.speaker: James Lincoln Collier
about_quotes.evidence: if he hadn’t been a hanging judge Arthur Miller never would have been able to write The Crucible.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Lincoln Collier interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Yusef Lateef
about_quotes.evidence: As a result, I’ve written...two or three plays, and I’ve written a novella, a book of short stories, as a result of him inspiring me and motivating me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Yusef Lateef — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Arthur Miller was going to [corrected to Arthur Mitchell]... Not Arthur Miller.
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
